calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-16-2026 Continued To Jul-01-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Stay Of The Proceedings Pending Appeal
Off Calendar Mar-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CODDINGTON, HICKS & DANFORTH; RANDOLPH S. HICKS; HENRY GREGORY NELCH; DANIEL SHEVTSOV; AND MAHMOUD A. FADLI'S MOTION FOR STAY OF THE PROCEEDINGS PENDING APPEAL

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The case is stayed for all purposes until resolution of the pending appeal in Alsaedi et al. v. Westbrook et al., First District Court of Appeal, Case No. A170425.

This case arises out of a personal injury lawsuit that resulted in a large judgment against plaintiff Joshua Westbook. The judgment is currently on appeal.

"[A] court ordinarily has inherent power, in its discretion, to stay proceedings when such a stay will accommodate the ends of justice." (St. Paul Fire and Marine Insurance Company v. AmerisourceBergen Corporation (2022) 80 Cal.App.5th 1, 6-7 [citation simplified].) A stay in this case is appropriate, as the disposition of the appeal directly impacts a determination of causation and damages and may prevent simultaneous and duplicative litigation. (See Beal Bank, SSB v. Arter & Hadden, LLP (2007) 42 Cal.4th 503, 512-514; Jordache Enterprises, Inc. v. Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal. 4th 739, 750, 758.) The court is unpersuaded by Plaintiff's argument that the stay will substantially prejudice him through a loss of evidence. There is no realistic prospect of lost evidence here. The court takes judicial notice sua sponte of the docket in Case No. A170425 and notes that the appeal is fully briefed.

The court observes that Westbrook's FAC changed the case caption, renaming "Progressive Casualty Insurance Company" (from the original complaint) as "Drive New Jersey Insurance Company" (in the FAC). No party objected, but plaintiff is cautioned that a court order is required to change the case caption, and a filing that contains new parties without court order may be stricken. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 473, subd. (a)(1) [court may allow amendment to correct a mistake in the name of a party].) In future filings, the parties should include the original party names but may include the corrected party name as well (e.g. "Drive New Jersey Insurance Company erroneously sued as Progressive Casualty Insurance Company").

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
